en.description_en: This wooden standing statue of the Eleven-Headed Kannon Bodhisattva, serving as a front image, is a sculpture in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1993-08-25. It is located in Hatcho.

en.description_en: This wooden standing statue of Amida Nyorai (Amitabha Buddha) is a sculpture in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1997-10-01. It is located in Shinchi.

en.description_en: This wooden standing statue of Yakushi Nyorai (Medicine Buddha) is a sculpture in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 2003-04-24. It is located in Numamori.

en.description_en: This wooden standing statue of Jizo Bodhisattva is a sculpture in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 2003-04-24. It is located in Kuriyama.

This lotus-form altar platform is a craftwork in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1978-04-01. It is located in Hatcho.
This cast bronze bell is a craftwork in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1978-04-01. It is located in Nozume.

This banner of Shinmeigu Shrine is a Tangible Cultural Property classified under the documents subcategory. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1992-02-27. It is held at the History and Folklore Museum.
The Masuga Diary is an old document in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1992-02-27. It is located in Sugaya.

en.description_en: This vermilion-seal charter together with a map of the vermilion-seal land is an old document in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1994-01-24. It is located in Nozume.

en.name_en: Artifacts Excavated from the Ota Tumulus (Grave Goods including a Bronze Mirror)
en.description_en: These artifacts excavated from the Ota tumulus, including grave goods such as a bronze mirror, are archaeological material in the Tangible Cultural Property category. They are town-designated by Yachiyo Town and were designated on 2019-01-25. They are held at the History and Folklore Museum.

This stone stupa tablet dated to the 11th month of Bunna 4 (1355) is archaeological material in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1978-04-01. It is located in Arai.

This stone stupa tablet dated to the 4th month of Koan 6 (1283) is archaeological material in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1978-04-01. It is located in Setoi.

This stone stupa tablet dated to the 4th month of Bunpo 2 (1318) is archaeological material in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1978-04-01. It is located in Arai.

This Thirteen Buddhas stone stupa tablet is archaeological material in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 2019-01-25. It is held at the History and Folklore Museum.
